[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)

- Description: GAS final mirror mint + identity pinning (PR-1g + PR-1h)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/326

  Details:
  GAS final mirror mint (PR-1h) + identity pinning generalised (PR-1g consolidated), 2026-05-29.
  
  Closes the last 'unmirrored sources' pre-flight item from PR-1b. Audit goes from 36/13/1 to 37/13/0.
  
  PRs delivered:
  
  1. tokenomics#326 — PR-1h. Gary shared 1zKgMwd6 (admin@truesight.me-owned Gmail digital-signature ingestion) from the admin@ GAS UI with garyjob@agroverse.shop at Editor level. clasp clone succeeded under gary@'s current clasp identity without any clasp login dance. Mirror at clasp_mirrors/1zKgMwd6.../ with appsscript.json + Code.js + Credentials.js (cloned by clasp) + Version.gs (added by ensure_clasp_version_gs.mjs). Only .clasp.json tracked per the gitignore convention. PR-1g's identity-pinning check still active — deploy_gas_project.py --push refuses against this scriptId under gary@'s identity because owner_email = admin@truesight.me. Future pushes require Path B per-account clasprc workflow or --allow-identity-mismatch override.
  
  2. agentic_ai_context#X — TOKENOMICS_GAS_RESTRUCTURE_PLAN.md resume tracker bumped: PR-1g and PR-1h marked ✅, RESUME HERE pointer updated to reflect zero unmirrored remaining.
  
  The why behind the share-not-login choice: investigated earlier in the session why an apparent 'prior Claude pushed as admin@' worked, and the most likely explanation is shared-access pattern (admin@ historically shares many of its scripts with gary@; gary@'s clasp identity can see 57 scripts including most admin@-owned ones). 1zKgMwd6 was the unique unsharedness. One click in the admin@ Share dialog resolved it.
  
  Path B per-account clasprc setup is still pending Gary's browser dance (one-time clasp login as each account, save clasprc files). Once done, future admin@ pushes are a simple cp ~/.clasprc-admin.json ~/.clasprc.json before deploy_gas_project.py --push.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
